financial.note: A key financier for renewable projects; while providing favorable terms, the company's high Net D/E (~1.8x in 2026) makes it sensitive to global credit tightening.
Provides working capital and project finance; BGRIM's high leverage ratio translates to significant interest expense sensitivity during rate hikes.

supply_chain.note: Natural gas accounts for ~70% of BGRIM's cost of goods sold; price spikes in pooled gas or LNG imports directly compress SPP margins.
The largest customer under long-term PPAs; revenue is highly dependent on EGAT's dispatch volume and the regulated Ft rate.
Direct electricity and steam sales to industrial clients; margins are higher than EGAT sales but dependent on domestic manufacturing activity.
Direct electricity and steam sales to industrial clients; margins are higher than EGAT sales but dependent on domestic manufacturing activity.

competitors.note: Direct competition for PDP (Power Development Plan) quotas and industrial client poaching.
Rivalry in the SPP (Small Power Producer) segment and industrial steam supply.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: The regulated mechanism to pass through fuel costs; a rising Ft rate increases revenue, though usually with a regulatory lag.
Reflects the cost of marginal gas supply for power generation; high spot prices correlate with margin squeeze in the SPP segment.
Dual impact: Increases the cost of imported LNG and creates unrealized FX losses on USD-denominated debt.
Impacts the cost of refinancing large debt tranches and reduces the valuation of long-term cash flow assets (DCF sensitivity).
